reasoning: The baseline reference value for Canada over the preceding 360 days was 1.083, which corresponds to exactly 1 explosion/remote violence event over the entire 360-day period (1 + 1/12 = 1.083). For any of the resolution dates to resolve as YES, there would need to be strictly more than 10.83 events, meaning at least 11 events must occur within a single 30-day window. Canada is extremely politically stable with virtually no history of frequent remote violence or explosions (save for very rare, isolated incidents). Achieving 11 such events within 30 days would require an unprecedented security crisis, such as a large-scale terror campaign or an intense, highly unusual gang war involving explosives. Given the base rate of ~1 event per year, the likelihood of 11 events in a month is vanishingly small. I am assigning the near-minimum allowed probabilities, increasing slightly for later periods to reflect horizon uncertainty.
